description: Committed retrieval-quality baseline (hit@1/hit@5/MRR) produced by scripts/retrieval-eval.py over wiki-grounding.py rank. Addresses review finding F5 and its scale-up follow-up — the labelled set is now scaled from 12 to 31 queries (12 question-node-seeded + 19 authored held-out queries with hand-validated ground truth), giving a statistically firmer before/after for the deferred retrieval redesign.

harness.metric_ground_truth: the committed per-base eval-set files references/retrieval-eval-set-<base>.json (expected_slugs = the hand-validated answering source-page set per query); the seeded subset originates from each base's wiki/questions/*.md sources_answering:

corpus.note: Eval corpus is the bound alpha knowledge bases under .alpha/ — these are gitignored local alpha data, NOT version-controlled in this repo. The numbers below are a snapshot measured against those bases (the maintainer-accepted Option-1 reproducibility posture: the committed eval-set files document the labelled queries + ground truth; exact reproduction requires the same .alpha/ bases on disk). See retrieval-baseline.md.
corpus.bases_qualifying_reason: only these bound bases carry source corpora large enough to author a labelled eval set against

scale_note: 31 labelled queries (12 question-node-seeded + 19 authored held-out). Meets the brief's aspired ~20-50 range. The seeded subset reproduces the prior 12-query baseline (e.g. eu-ai-act-sme still 3/6 null on hit@5), confirming harness stability across the scale-up. Reproducibility decision: Option 1 (alpha-corpus snapshot).

per_query.query: EU AI Act Anwendungsbereich KMU: Wer ist Anbieter, wer Betreiber, und welche kleinen und mittleren Unternehmen sind betroffen?
EU AI Act Fristen und Inkrafttreten: gestaffelte Geltungsbeginn-Termine 2025, 2026, 2027 für die einzelnen Pflichten
EU AI Act KI-Kompetenz (AI literacy) und Pflichten für General-Purpose-AI (GPAI): was müssen KMU beachten, die KI-Modelle wie ChatGPT einsetzen?
EU AI Act Pflichten für Hochrisiko-KI-Systeme: Konformitätsbewertung, Risikomanagement, Dokumentation und menschliche Aufsicht für Anbieter und Betreiber
EU AI Act Risikoklassifizierung: verbotene Praktiken, Hochrisiko-KI, begrenztes und minimales Risiko – wie ordnen KMU ihre KI-Systeme ein?
EU AI Act praktische Umsetzung für KMU: Governance-Schritte, Sanktionen und KMU-Erleichterungen wie Reallabore und reduzierte Gebühren
Welche Sanktionen und Bußgelder drohen bei Verstößen gegen die KI-Verordnung?
What are the human oversight requirements for high-risk AI systems under Article 14?
Welche technische Dokumentation müssen Anbieter von Hochrisiko-KI nach Artikel 11 erstellen?
What is a regulatory sandbox under the AI Act and how can SMEs benefit (Article 57)?
What conformity assessment procedure is required for high-risk AI systems under Article 43?
Which AI practices are prohibited under Article 5 of the AI Act?
How does the Digital Omnibus change the EU AI Act deadlines for the Mittelstand?
What systems are classified as high-risk in Annex III of the AI Act?
How are key terms like 'AI system' defined in Article 3 of the AI Act?
EU Cyber Resilience Act core manufacturer obligations: essential cybersecurity requirements, conformity assessment, CE marking, vulnerability handling and security updates
EU Cyber Resilience Act scope: which products with digital elements and which manufacturers fall in scope, and how do micro/small/medium enterprise provisions apply
EU Cyber Resilience Act timelines, incident and vulnerability reporting duties to ENISA, and penalties for non-compliance
What penalties and fines can be imposed for non-compliance with the Cyber Resilience Act (Article 64)?
What vulnerability and incident reporting obligations does CRA Article 14 impose on manufacturers?
What conformity assessment procedures apply to products with digital elements under the CRA?
How do CRA requirements map to harmonised cybersecurity standards?
What is the implementation timeline and transition pathway for the Cyber Resilience Act?
EU Cyber Resilience Act core manufacturer obligations: essential cybersecurity requirements, conformity assessment, CE marking, vulnerability handling and security updates
EU Cyber Resilience Act scope: which products with digital elements and which manufacturers fall in scope, and how do micro/small/medium enterprise provisions apply
EU Cyber Resilience Act timelines, incident and vulnerability reporting duties to ENISA, and penalties for non-compliance
What must manufacturers do to obtain CE marking under the Cyber Resilience Act?
What vulnerability handling obligations does Annex I Part II of the CRA require?
